sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 4 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN, DOWN, RIGHT, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 41, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED step 3)', 'LEFT': 'Moves active object left 2px within container (VERIFIED step 4)', 'UP': "Moves up within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block only at specific 'portal' position (cols 10-11, rows 40-41) (VERIFIED steps 5,13,17)", 'DOWN': 'Moves down within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block at portal position (VERIFIED step 6)', 'MOUSE': 'Does NOT change active object selection (VERIFIED step 9)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'green_pixel': 'Active player object, 2x2, always the controlled entity', 'divider_blocks': 'White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, rows 10-50 in vertical line', 'gray_containers': 'Irregular gray boxes on left panel holding colored pixels', 'white_containers': 'White frames on right panel containing colored shapes (red and blue)', 'colored_pixels': 'Small 2x2 objects inside containers: yellow, blue, red, dark red, green'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'White horizontal bar at row 63, shrinks as timer/progress indicator', 'divider': 'Vertical line of white 2x2 blocks at col 32-33'}
Current level:
- World model: Two-panel layout. Left (charcoal bg) has gray containers with colored 2x2 pixels. Right (black bg) has white containers with red/blue shapes. Green pixel is the active object. Container has narrow part (cols 8-11) and wide part (cols 8-13). UP/DOWN in narrow part at rows 40-41 swaps with divider blocks. UP/DOWN in wide part moves within container. UP at cols 8-9 or cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does nothing. Green currently at [[38,10],[39,11]].
- Goal model: Move green pixel through divider to right side to match targets in white containers. The 'portal' for swapping with divider is at cols 10-11, rows 40-41.
- Recent findings: ['UP swap with divider works only at cols 10-11, rows 40-41', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does NOT swap (step 17)', 'UP at cols 8-9 does NOT swap (step 16)', 'UP at cols 12-13 moves within container (step 12)']
- Open questions: ["What is the exact 'portal' position for divider swap?", 'How to move green pixel to right side after swapping?', 'What is the target configuration?']
- Plan: Move green DOWN to rows 40-41, keep at cols 10-11, then UP to swap with divider. From divider, try RIGHT to move to right side.
- Strategy log: ['Tried UP from various positions in narrow container part - only rows 40-41 at cols 10-11 triggers swap']
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE(20,24) on yellow pixel: no selection change (step 9)', 'UP at cols 8-9 rows 38-39: no change (step 16)', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39: no change (step 17)', 'RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no movement (step 13)', 'UP at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no change (step 12)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 40 of about 59 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
No transitions caused board changes! This means the world model's claim about "UP swap with divider works only at cols 10-11, rows 40-41 (VERIFIED steps 5,13,17)" is WRONG. No action has ever caused a board change.
This is a crucial finding. The game might not be about moving the green pixel at all. Maybe it's about MOUSE clicks, or maybe the goal is to arrange something differently.
Let me reconsider the game. The board has:
- Left panel: three containers with colored pixels
- Right panel: two containers with colored shapes
- A divider in the middle
Maybe the game is about MOUSE clicking on objects to "activate" them. The green pixel might not be a "player" but rather an indicator of which container is currently active.
Let me try clicking on the green pixel with MOUSE to see if it does something different now. Or maybe I should click on the colored shapes on the right side.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the problem. The game says "Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE." If no movement action has ever caused a board change, maybe the game is purely about MOUSE interactions.
Let me try MOUSE on different objects:
1. The green pixel itself
2. The red shape on the right
3. The blue shape on the right
4. The divider blocks
Let me start by trying MOUSE on the green pixel to see if it does something.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# CRITICAL: No action has ever caused a board change!
# The world model about "portal swap" is WRONG.
# Let me try MOUSE on different objects to find the actual mechanic.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
# Find green pixel position
green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
print(f"Green: {green['boundary']}")

# Find red shape on right
red_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R']
print(f"Red nodes: {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in red_nodes]}")

# Find blue shape on right
blue_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
print(f"Blue nodes: {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in blue_nodes]}")

# Try MOUSE on the green pixel center
green_center_row = sum(p[0] for p in green['boundary']) // 4
green_center_col = sum(p[1] for p in green['boundary']) // 4
print(f"Trying MOUSE on green at ({green_center_row}, {green_center_col})")

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': green_center_row, 'col': green_center_col}])
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}")

# Check if anything changed
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
green2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
print(f"Green after MOUSE: {green2['boundary']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves green right 2px within container", "LEFT": "Moves green left 2px within container", "UP": "Moves green up within container if space", "DOWN": "Moves green down within container if space", "MOUSE": "Unknown effect - need to test on different targets"}, "object_taxonomy": {"green_pixel": "Active 2x2 object (green=N)", "divider_blocks": "White 2x2 at col 32-33", "colored_pixels": "2x2 objects in left containers: yellow, blue, red, dark red, green"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "White bar row 63"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "CRITICAL FINDING: No action has ever caused board_changed=True. The 'portal swap' theory is WRONG. Green moves within container but no board change occurs. Game might be MOUSE-based.", "goal_model": "Unknown - need to find what actually changes the board", "recent_findings": ["No transition has board_changed=True", "Green moves within container but board never changes"], "open_questions": ["What causes board changes? Is it MOUSE on specific objects?"], "current_plan": "Try MOUSE on green pixel, then on colored shapes on right", "strategy_log": ["Movement actions don't cause board changes - need to find real mechanic"], "failed_probes": ["All movement actions: no board change"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
